concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle.

concepts.name: FIFO Principle
concepts.short_definition: The first element added to the queue is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Enqueue Operation
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding an element to the rear of the queue.

concepts.name: Dequeue Operation
concepts.short_definition: The process of removing an element from the front of the queue.

concepts.name: Front Element
concepts.short_definition: The element at the front of the queue, which is the next to be dequeued.

concepts.name: Rear Element
concepts.short_definition: The element at the rear of the queue, where new elements are enqueued.

concepts.name: Empty Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue that contains no elements.

concepts.name: Full Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue that has reached its maximum capacity (relevant for fixed-size implementations).

concepts.name: Queue Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Different methods for realizing a queue data structure, such as using arrays or linked lists.

concepts.name: Queue Applications
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and algorithms where queues are effectively utilized.
